steps.title: RabbitMQ and Go Predict Delayed Retry Vs Ttl · Theory Delayed Retry Vs Ttl
steps.instruction:
theory case — A theory Go messaging team handles case 4314 during quorum failover exercise during a rolling broker restart. Build deterministic Go evidence to enforce RabbitMQ 4.3 delayed retry, strict priority, and consumer-timeout policy limits; reject stale connection and channel generations; then lose a quorum member and recompute majority availability. Browser code uses pure values and state transitions only. amqp091-go, AMQP sockets, RabbitMQ, TLS, partitions, restarts, Docker, databases, race and leak checks, load, upgrades, and production behavior require explicit authorized transfer gates.

For RabbitMQ and Go Defend the Delayed Retry Vs Ttl model · Theory Delayed Retry Vs Ttl, challenge the documented misconception with runtime, failure, race, or toolchain evidence: TTL dead-letter cycles and native delayed retry have identical safety and resource behavior. In Go, a new broker policy can be assumed safe for existing Go consumers without a compatibility drill.
